findings.message: `JsonConfigBranchStrategyAdapter::new` reads the config path directly with `read_to_string` and never rejects symlinks or verifies containment under a trusted root. A symlinked `.harness/config/branch-strategy.json` can therefore redirect this adapter to attacker-controlled content outside the repository, which matches the symlink-boundary issue this layer is supposed to fail closed on.

A symlinked `track/items/<id>` or `track/archive/<id>` directory is accepted here because `entry.path().is_dir()` follows symlinks, and the later `metadata.json` / `impl-plan.json` / `task-coverage.json` reads never re-check that the resolved path stays under `root`. A repo-controlled symlink can therefore escape the trusted root and feed external files into the render pipeline.

`all_track_directories()` follows symlinked `track/items/<id>` / `track/archive/<id>` entries with `is_dir()`, and `load_track_metadata()` then reads `metadata.json` and `impl-plan.json` from that symlink target without a containment check. That lets the latest-track verifier select and parse data outside `root` if a track directory is replaced with a symlink.

Leaf symlinks are still followed when loading track state for latest-track/freshness checks. `latest_track.rs` reads `metadata.json` directly at line 254, `track/render/snapshot.rs` reads `impl-plan.json` directly at line 66, and `verify/view_freshness.rs` reads `metadata.json` directly at line 173. Those paths never do the same `symlink_metadata()` + reject step used elsewhere in the repo, so a symlinked file inside an otherwise real track directory can redirect these checks to content outside `track/items`.

`sync_rendered_views` still accepts symlinked track inputs. `catalogue_path_exists()` explicitly counts symlinks as present, and the render path then reads `spec.json` at line 180 and `*-types.json` at line 325 with `read_to_string()`. That lets a symlink under `track/items/<id>` feed arbitrary outside JSON into generated views instead of enforcing real-file semantics.

The new `metadata_path.is_file()` pre-check runs before `reject_symlinks_below`, so a dangling symlink or other non-regular-file `metadata.json` is treated as absent and silently skipped instead of being rejected at the boundary. That bypasses the fail-closed guard that follows and can hide corrupted track state.

`resolve_diff_base` builds `track_dir.join(".commit_hash")` and hands it to `FsDryCheckCommitHashStore` without first proving the resolved path remains under `canonical_root`. The store only rejects symlinks before `read_to_string`; it does not canonicalize and enforce `starts_with(canonical_root)`, so a path-traversing `track_dir` supplied at this adapter boundary can make the dry-gate read a `.commit_hash` outside the trusted repo root. This should fail closed using the existing trusted-root containment pattern (`is_safe_briefing_path`/CN-04 style) before any file read or fallback decision.

`resolve_context` canonicalizes `--items-dir` but never rejects a symlinked root before the trust check. That lets a symlinked `items_dir` be redirected to a different tree, which is contrary to the existing symlink-rejection pattern used for trust-rooted filesystem inputs. Reject the root symlink before canonicalizing it.

`read_base_branch` reads `<canonical_items_dir>/<track_id>/metadata.json` with `std::fs::read_to_string` directly, so a symlinked `metadata.json` can redirect the load outside the track directory and feed an arbitrary base branch into fixpoint resolution. Existing infrastructure loaders reject symlinked metadata paths before reading; this one should too.

`FsDryCheckConfigLoaderAdapter` builds `.harness/config/dry-check.json` under `repo_root` and immediately delegates to `InfraDryCheckConfig::load`, whose file read follows symlinks. Unlike the existing `scope_config_loader.rs` canonicalize + `reject_symlinks_below` pattern, this boundary does not reject a symlinked config file or parent, so fixpoint-resolve can read dry-check settings from outside the trusted checkout and change or disable the gate instead of failing closed. Add canonical containment and symlink rejection against `repo_root` before loading.

`FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter` derives `dry-check.json` and `dry-check-coverage.json` with `track_dir.join(...)` and then constructs filesystem stores without first proving the resolved paths remain below `canonical_root`. The downstream symlink guard rejects symlinks but does not reject paths outside the trusted root, so a caller that passes an absolute or `..`-based `track_dir` can bind the approval store to files outside the repository. This should fail closed with the same trusted-root containment discipline used by the `is_safe_briefing_path`/CN-04 path policy and the sibling `trusted_commit_hash_path` helper before constructing the stores.

`briefing_file` validation here still accepts Windows drive-relative paths such as `C:review.md`. `FilePath::new()` only rejects absolute and `..` traversal, so `trusted_root.join(briefing)` can discard `trusted_root` on Windows and resolve the briefing outside the workspace. Add a post-join `starts_with(trusted_root)` check or tighten the domain validator to reject drive-relative prefixes.

D1 and D4 define conflicting sources of truth for post-init track operations. D1 says the strategy is loaded from `.harness/config/branch-strategy.json` via `BranchStrategyPort` and the replacement table routes PR creation and branch operations through that port, but D4 says PR creation, commit gates, and branch operations must use the immutable `metadata.json` snapshot after track init and not the global config. The plan needs one coherent contract here; otherwise an implementation can still let an in-flight track change behavior when the global config changes, defeating D4's stated invariant.

D8 is internally inconsistent about the dogfooding/bootstrap sequence. The rationale says this ADR implementation track itself is the first develop-based track, but the explicit bootstrap steps say this same implementation track starts from the current main base, merges to main, creates develop only after that, and only subsequent new tracks are develop-based. This also leaves the current pre-implementation track outside the claimed dogfooding path, so the plan should clarify whether dogfooding starts with this track or with the first track after develop is created.

The Related section labels `.github/workflows/ci.yml` as out of scope/user responsibility, but D8 explicitly includes updating SoTOHE-core's own workflow to `branches: [main, develop]`, and the Out of Scope section separately limits the exclusion to user repository CI YAML rewriting. As written, this cross-reference can misroute implementation planning by treating the SoTOHE-core workflow update as excluded when D8 requires it.

D1 allows `merge_target` to differ from `base_branch` and defines `merge_target` as the PR base, but this line then routes the ADR lifecycle checks (`git log main` replacement) through `base_branch()`. For any supported configuration where those fields diverge, an ADR already merged to the PR target can still be absent from the branch used for the check, so the adr-editor/pre-merge logic can misclassify immutable post-merge ADRs as editable. The ADR should either use `merge_target()` for merge-state checks or explicitly forbid divergence for workflows that depend on ADR immutability.

The bootstrap sequence says this implementation track starts under the current main-based system and then ships the fail-closed snapshot requirement, while D5 says all pre-implementation tracks without `branch_strategy_snapshot` parse-fail and receive no remediation. The current track metadata in this review has no snapshot, so once the parser is made fail-closed, the commands needed to finish this same transition track can no longer resolve it unless the plan adds an explicit bootstrap exception or requires snapshotting this track before enforcement.

D7's frontmatter records the selected candidate as `base-branch-via-port`, but the ADR body explicitly says the adr-editor pre/post-merge check must use `merge_target()` because it is checking whether the ADR has landed on the PR base. That leaves two conflicting design signals for the same decision; an implementer following the frontmatter could wire adr-editor to the base branch and misclassify merged ADRs when `base_branch` and `merge_target` differ. Align the D7 candidate label with the body, e.g. `merge-target-via-port`.

`JsonConfigBranchStrategyAdapter::new` is specified as infallible (`-> Self`) even though it reads and parses `.harness/config/branch-strategy.json` during construction. That leaves no way to propagate missing/invalid config through `TrackCompositionRoot::track_init`; the only outcomes are a panic or an implicit fallback, both of which violate the repo’s error-handling rule. Make the constructor fallible and thread that error through the wiring task.

IN-10/AC-15 make post-merge `develop` branch creation part of the tracked implementation and `task-coverage.json` maps it to T012, but T012 only edits config/CI/docs/metadata and cannot create or push a remote branch before the PR is merged. This leaves the plan with an acceptance criterion no implementation task can satisfy inside the PR; branch creation should be kept as post-merge ops outside task coverage, or modeled as an explicit post-merge gate.

AC-07 says the updated metadata codec must not accept input containing `kind`, but the parenthetical allows either `deny_unknown_fields` or field ignoring. Ignoring unknown fields would accept a `kind`-bearing payload, which contradicts the criterion and leaves implementers unable to tell whether D3 requires rejecting `kind` on read or only omitting it on write.

`EnsurePrArgs` says the composition root should resolve the PR `base` argument from `base_branch`, but the ADR defines `merge_target` as the PR base and T011 also routes PR creation through `port.merge_target()`. If this catalogue wording drives implementation or tests, configurations where `base_branch != merge_target` will open PRs against the wrong branch.

CN-05 requires the adr-editor briefing to include the resolved `merge_target`, but the only covered task, T014, rewrites the prompt to consume that value without adding any task to compute or pass it in the briefing. Implementing this plan as written leaves the prompt depending on an undefined variable and does not satisfy D7/CN-05.

D1 first defines the abstraction as `BranchStrategyPort`, but this passage switches to passing a `BranchStrategy` value into the usecase layer. The spec and usecase catalogue only define `BranchStrategyPort`, so the ADR is internally inconsistent about whether the boundary is a port trait or a value object.

`TrackDocumentV2` says the missing `branch_strategy_snapshot` field will fail parsing "via serde deny_unknown_fields", but that attribute only rejects unknown extra keys, not absent required fields. The artifact is misdescribing the mechanism that enforces CN-03; it should say the field is non-optional and missing data fails because the DTO requires it.

`task-coverage.json` omits `T015` from `IN-05`, even though `IN-05` explicitly includes the `.claude/commands/track/done.md` and `.claude/rules/07-dev-environment.md` updates that are implemented in `T015`. That leaves the coverage artifact internally inconsistent with the plan and can make the branch-switch rename scope look fully covered when part of it is not mapped.

D1 defines `merge_method` as the default PR merge method and the shipped config sets it to `squash`, but the implementation plan only persists/exposes `merge_method` and wires PR creation to `merge_target`; the only `.claude/commands/track/merge.md` task updates next-action text. No task or AC routes `merge_method()` into `sotp pr wait-and-merge` or the track merge default, so this plan can pass while ignoring the configured merge method.

`task-coverage.json` maps AC-07 to T003, but T003 only adds the required `branch_strategy_snapshot`, bumps schema_version, and tests the missing-snapshot parse error. AC-07 also requires rejecting payloads that still contain a `kind` field and confirming generated metadata omits `kind`; with the current task text, that acceptance criterion is not actually planned.

T005 is the task that wires the branch strategy into track initialization, but `task-contract.json` maps it to an empty contract list. The task explicitly changes `TrackServiceImpl` to receive `BranchStrategyPort`, construct a `BranchStrategySnapshot`, and write it through `TrackMetadata`; those relevant contract entries already exist in the catalogues. Leaving T005 unbound breaks the spec → catalogue → implementation-plan chain, so the pre-review task-contract gate cannot enforce the key CN-02/AC-08 behavior for the task that actually implements it.

`MergeMethodDocument` is specified as a serde enum with `Squash | Merge | Rebase`, while the required config and metadata JSON literals use lowercase `"squash"`. Unless the plan explicitly requires lowercase serde renames/custom parsing, the DTO described here will not parse the JSON mandated by AC-01/T012/ADR D1, so the bootstrap config and self-applied metadata are internally inconsistent.

T010 conflates `base_branch` with the PR base argument. The ADR explicitly defines `merge_target` as the PR base and allows it to diverge from `base_branch`, and `cli-types.json` documents `EnsurePrArgs.base` as resolving from `port.merge_target()`. This task then says branch_ops should replace `current != "main"` comparisons with `base_branch` values injected from `EnsurePrArgs`, which would use the merge target as the base-branch guard whenever those two differ. The plan should route branch operation guards through the snapshot/port `base_branch()` only, not through `EnsurePrArgs.base`.

The plan introduces user-configured branch names as only `NonEmptyString` values, but then directs T006 to build a `git rev-parse` command with `format!("git rev-parse {base_branch}")`. Because `.harness/config/branch-strategy.json` is external input, this leaves illegal or shell-significant branch names representable and instructs implementers to interpolate them into a command string. The plan needs a validated branch-ref domain type and/or an explicit argv-style command contract before these values reach git invocation sites.

ADR D1 still tells implementers to change the `git rev-parse main` fallback to `format!("git rev-parse {}", strategy.base_branch())`, but the spec/impl-plan require configured branch names to be passed as separate argv entries and never interpolated into shell command text. This is a semantic contradiction in the plan artifacts and can lead the implementation to violate AC-04's command-boundary safety requirement.

T010 explicitly includes changing `WaitAndMergeArgs.method` from a hardcoded default to `Option<String>` so the configured merge method can be used, but the task-contract entry for T010 maps only `TrackCommand` and `EnsurePrArgs`, and the CLI catalogue has no `WaitAndMergeArgs` contract entry. That leaves a required public CLI shape change outside the type-contract gate, so the plan's task-to-contract mapping is incomplete for the merge-method default behavior.

`task-coverage.json` maps `IN-06` only to `T001` and `T005`, but `spec.json` says `IN-06` also includes the `metadata.json` schema-version update. `T003` is the task that actually updates `TrackDocumentV2` / bumps `schema_version`, so leaving it out means the coverage gate does not cover the codec change that makes `branch_strategy_snapshot` usable.

`task-coverage.json` maps `IN-07` only to `T003`, but `spec.json` says `IN-07` also includes the D5 bootstrap exception that writes `branch_strategy_snapshot` into this track's own `metadata.json`. `T012` is the task that performs that self-application, so the scope map omits the task that actually satisfies the bootstrap exception.

`switch_base` is marked as having a default trait body here, but the surrounding plan treats it as real branch-switch behavior driven by the active track snapshot. That combination is inconsistent: a default implementation would let concrete `TrackService` implementers omit the operation entirely, yet no default behavior is specified anywhere in the plan and the doc comment says it should switch to the active track's base branch.

Marking `TrackService::switch_base` as `has_default_impl: true` in [usecase-types.json](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/track/items/branch-strategy-config-driven-2026-06-30/usecase-types.json#L281) turns the new service API into an optional hook: a `TrackService` impl can now omit the override and still compile, with the failure only surfacing at runtime. That contradicts the surrounding plan in [plan.md](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/track/items/branch-strategy-config-driven-2026-06-30/plan.md#L8), which treats `switch_base` as part of the compile-stable foundation for the branch-strategy flow.

`switch_base` is only given a temporary failure default here, but no later task actually adds a concrete `TrackService` implementation that overrides it. `T011` wires the call path and branch-strategy adapters, so as written the new `SwitchBase` flow will still invoke the placeholder and never reach real branch-switch behavior.

`TrackServiceImpl::switch_base` is specified to delegate upward to `TrackCompositionRoot::track_switch_base`, which inverts the dependency direction between the service/infrastructure layer and the CLI composition root. That creates a layer cycle and cannot be implemented as written; the composition root should call the service, not the other way around.

S7 says "All changes are text replacements; no behavioral logic changes," but T015.5 explicitly removes the hardcoded merge-commit default and changes the omitted-method behavior of `bin/sotp pr wait-and-merge`. That is a behavioral change, so the section summary is inaccurate.

T011 is infeasible as written: it places the shared `switch-base` helper in `apps/cli-composition`, then requires `libs/infrastructure`'s `TrackServiceImpl::switch_base` to use that same helper. That forces an upward dependency from infrastructure into the composition-root crate and creates a layering cycle unless the helper is moved into a lower-level shared module.

`BranchStrategyConfigError` is documented as enforcing fail-closed behavior “per CN-03”, but the task text for the same adapter error type assigns that no-fallback/no-grace-period requirement to D5, while CN-03 is used for the metadata missing-field fail-closed behavior. This leaves the type catalogue pointing readers at the wrong design decision for config parse/read failures; update the reference to D5 or otherwise align the catalogue with T004.

`T003` still describes the codec change as bumping `schema_version` from `1 -> 2`, but this track artifact is already at `schema_version: 6`, and the briefing for this batch says the metadata transition is `v5 -> v6`. That makes the plan internally inconsistent about the version being introduced. The same wording is repeated in the rendered plan view.

T011 makes `track_init` fail closed on `.harness/config/branch-strategy.json`, but that file is only introduced later in T012. As ordered, the init path is broken until the later section lands, so the plan needs an explicit dependency or T012 moved ahead of T011.

`OS-07` says the `apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/` diff-base / agent-profile logic is out of scope, but `IN-04` still explicitly includes `apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/shared.rs` in the `git rev-parse main` fallback replacement set. As written, the spec both includes and excludes the same subtree, so it is unclear whether `review_v2/shared.rs` is meant to change in this track.

T022 and T023 explicitly require moving dry write/check-approved rendering into `apps/cli-driver/src/dry.rs`, but the task contract attributes both tasks only to `cli_composition:DryCompositionRoot`, and `cli_driver-types.json` contains no `DryDriver` entry representing the new renderer responsibility. This leaves AC-18's cli-driver render migration outside the catalogue/task contract, so the plan can appear covered while omitting the artifact that is supposed to own the behavior.

IN-12/AC-17 state that fixpoint-resolve invoke and render move to `apps/cli-driver` and that the composition-root successor should not directly call the use case interactor, but T019 instructs a new `apps/cli-composition` adapter to construct and invoke `FixpointResolveInteractor`. Those are conflicting implementation targets: following T019 keeps the old interactor invocation in cli-composition, while following AC-17 requires the driver to own invoke+render.

OS-07 is internally inconsistent: it is listed under `out_of_scope`, but its first sentence says the `review_v2/shared.rs` `build_v2_shared` / `resolve_diff_base` base-branch parameterization "is within this track's scope". That leaves the scope boundary for IN-04 ambiguous.

`GO-03` is attached to the wrong task set: it says the ADR 1328 layer-splitting cleanup is what `T006〜T008 (IN-04)` changes, but those tasks are the `git rev-parse main` fallback/fixture edits. The actual ADR 1328 split work is grouped under `S7` / `T016〜T023`, so this goal text mislabels the plan and makes the goal-to-task mapping internally inconsistent. `spec.md` repeats the same mismatch at line 13.

T019 moves the actual `FixpointResolveService::resolve` invocation and surrounding orchestration into an infrastructure `SecondaryAdapter`, while IN-12/AC-17 require the fixpoint-resolve invoke+render path to live in `cli_driver` and ADR D4 puts application orchestration in usecase. The planned `FixpointResolveDriverService` becomes only a pass-through to a secondary port whose adapter invokes the real use case, so implementing this plan would shift the invoke/orchestration leak out of `cli_composition` rather than satisfying the stated layer split.

The task order breaks the staged-compatibility claim for S7: T018 first changes `TrackDriver::new` to require a second service, T019 then deletes `FixpointResolveInput` and the `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` entrypoint, and only T020 updates the `apps/cli` caller. At the T019 boundary the surviving CLI still imports/calls symbols that T019 removed, so the T017->T018->T019->T020 chain is not executable as staged work despite CN-07/S7 claiming compatibility is preserved through the relocation.

`impl-plan.json` tells `FixpointResolveDriverInteractor` to change the process CWD itself around `dry_gate.resolve_dry_gate` (`set CWD to repo_root beforehand, restore ... afterward`). That puts process-global environment mutation into the usecase layer without any trait boundary, which conflicts with the repo’s layering rules and makes the new interactor depend on infrastructure concerns directly.

T017 asks for a `FixpointDryGateFactoryPort` port-failure test, but the same task defines `build(&self, base_branch) -> Arc<dyn FixpointDryGateService>` with no error channel, and the catalogue marks that build method infallible. Implementing this literally would require changing the declared port signature or leaving the requested error-path test impossible. If the intended coverage is a failing `FixpointDryGateService::resolve_dry_gate`, the task should say that instead.

T018 tells the new `FsFixpointWorkspaceContextAdapter` to fall back to hardcoded `"main"` when `<items>/<track_id>/metadata.json` is missing, but the same spec requires branch strategy to come from `metadata.json#branch_strategy_snapshot` after init and explicitly forbids compatibility/fallback handling for old or missing snapshot metadata. This would preserve a branch-strategy fallback in the new fixpoint-resolve path instead of failing closed or using the effective strategy, contradicting IN-06/IN-07/CN-03/CN-06 and undermining the branch-strategy migration plan.

P1: The plan is internally contradictory about how `FixpointWorkspaceContextPort::resolve_context` handles a missing track `metadata.json`. The type catalogue says the resolved `base_branch` falls back to `"main"` when `metadata.json` is absent, but T018 in `impl-plan.json` explicitly requires missing metadata and decode errors to become `FixpointWorkspaceContextError::Unavailable` with fail-closed behavior and no branch-name fallback. This is a semantic behavior choice, not a mechanical link check; leaving both instructions means the implementation can satisfy one artifact while violating the other and the track's fail-closed branch-strategy invariant.

The new S7 work is described as an ordered chain in the briefing and task text (`T017 -> T018 -> T019 -> T020` and `T021 -> T022/T023`), but `impl-plan.json` does not encode any of those prerequisites with machine-readable dependency edges. Every new task record in the S7 block has only `id`/`description`/`status`, so the scheduler can still run the later cutover tasks before their setup tasks. That makes the plan unenforceable as written.

The S7 summary says, "Every relocation in this section preserves existing CLI output text, exit codes, and test coverage; no behavior visible to users changes," but `T018` explicitly changes `FsFixpointWorkspaceContextAdapter` to fail closed and remove the previous branch-name fallback when `metadata.json` is missing or unreadable. If that path is reachable, the summary is false: the user-visible error/exit behavior does change.

The Batch D briefing says the plan-artifacts change is limited to T016-T023 status transitions and that earlier Batch C+ artifacts remain unchanged, but this diff also marks T009-T015 as done. That makes the rendered plan claim more work was completed in this batch than the review contract describes, so reviewers cannot distinguish the intended T016-T023 closure from off-scope task completion.

The plan artifacts are not limited to the Batch D status-only update described by the review brief: T010's description is rewritten to add cli-driver PrInput work, and the scoped diff also adds the PrInput catalogue/contract entries. That contradicts the stated plan-artifacts intent that existing task descriptions/catalogue/task-contract content remain unchanged except for T016-T023 completion state, so the batch's SoT is ambiguous unless these T010/PrInput edits are either removed from this batch or explicitly included in the plan intent.

T009-T015 are still marked `todo` here even though this integrated batch's plan artifacts already add the corresponding `cli_driver`/`cli`/`cli_composition` catalogue changes and the briefing says these S4/S5 items are part of the commit. `plan.md` mirrors the same stale state as 16/23 resolved, so the rendered plan is out of sync with the change set.

`PrInput` documents omitted `Ensure.base` as resolving to `branch_strategy_snapshot.base_branch`, but the related plan artifacts define `EnsurePrArgs.base` as the PR base / `merge_target` and T011 resolves PR ensure through `port.merge_target()`. Since `base_branch` and `merge_target` may intentionally differ, this is an internal contradiction that can steer the implementation/review toward PRs targeting the wrong branch. Update this text to say `merge_target / merge_method`.

The new happy-path test exercises `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` directly and only asserts the returned `ExitCode`; it never captures stdout/stderr from `execute_fixpoint_resolve`, so a regression in the CLI wrapper's emit path would still pass. This leaves the only bin-layer behavior in this handler effectively untested.

The PR handler now converts omitted `--base` and `--method` into an empty-string sentinel before calling the driver. That embeds the branch-strategy defaulting contract and the "empty is not a valid branch/method" invariant in the bin layer, instead of passing typed absence to `cli_driver` and letting driver/composition resolve it. This violates the CLI thin-bin policy from ADR D5/D8: keep this handler to parse-dispatch-emit and move the omission/default contract behind the driver boundary, e.g. with `Option` fields in `PrInput`.

This surviving comment still points readers to `apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs`, but this PR deletes that file. The same block later names a stale cli-composition test that no longer exists under that name/location, so the final branch leaves a dead reference in the CLI scope. Update the comment to the new test location or remove the path reference.

Adding `metadata.json` here makes the supposed empty-diff approval test stop exercising the Approved path. `GitDiffGetter` includes untracked files, and this helper does not exclude `items/test-track/metadata.json`, so the new file becomes a diff in the implicit `Other` scope. The `check_approved_approved_path_exits_success_with_ok_message` case now covers the bypass/success path instead of the intended empty-diff Approved case.

The new carve-out cites stale source locations: `DryDriverAdapter::dry_write` is at `apps/cli-composition/src/dry_driver_adapter.rs:55`, `DryCompositionRoot::dry_write` is at `apps/cli-composition/src/dry.rs:371`, and `DryCompositionRoot::dry_results` is at `apps/cli-composition/src/dry.rs:465`, not `dry_driver_adapter.rs:71`, `dry.rs:310`, and `dry.rs:404`. As written, reviewers will validate the wrong call sites and the exception will not reliably cover the intended request-time invokes.

Renaming the wrapper to `track-switch-base` breaks the shipped `/track:done` flow: `.claude/commands/track/done.md` still shells out to `cargo make track-switch-main`, so the command now points at a deleted task and will fail before it can switch branches. The README already advertises the new base-branch behavior, so the docs and executable surface are now out of sync.

The harness-policy adapter-SSoT check requires track command adapters to point at an existing `.harness/workflows/track/...` SSoT and avoid duplicating provider-independent workflow steps inline. This changed adapter still owns the `/track:done` step sequence directly (`Step 1`, `Step 2`, command invocation), and there is no referenced `.harness/workflows/track/done.md`, so the workflow can drift from other provider adapters instead of having a single SSoT.

`/track:done` now executes `cargo make track-switch-base`, but the command text still says it is returning/switching to `main` (also in the frontmatter and opening sentence). With the new branch strategy config using `develop` as the base branch, this makes the command report and reason about the wrong branch after execution.

These rules now advertise `track-switch-base` and `track-branch-create` as operating on the configured base branch, but the canonical branch strategy convention still defines the workflow around `main` as the base/fail-closed branch. The harness-policy severity policy requires convention changes to propagate consistently across `.claude/rules` and `knowledge/conventions`; otherwise agents reading both surfaces get contradictory branch preconditions for the same commands.

Phase 0 is still described as `main (direct)` in the phase invocation table, and the writer-ownership table repeats the same assumption later in the file. That contradicts the updated `init` workflow and branch-strategy convention, which now require the configured base branch, so the authoritative workflow docs are internally inconsistent on repos whose base branch is not `main`.

`Step 3` still writes `metadata.json` as `schema_version: 5` and only lists the identity fields. That contradicts the branch-strategy conventions, which require every track to freeze `metadata.json#branch_strategy_snapshot` at creation and then read that snapshot for post-init branch ops and ADR pre-merge detection. As written, a freshly initialized track will either never get the snapshot or will lose it if the branch-create step seeded one, so `track-switch-base` / PR merge / `git log <merge_target>` resolution will break or fall back incorrectly.

The post-merge handoff tells users to `git pull` on the merge target branch, but `/track:done` immediately switches to the configured base branch and the branch-strategy convention defines `track-switch-base` as the post-merge landing branch. If `base_branch` and `merge_target` ever diverge, this sends the user to the wrong local branch after a successful merge.

`.harness/config/branch-strategy.json` is still untracked (`git status --short` reports `??` and there is no cached diff), but this workflow now treats that path as the required source for the configured base branch. Because the harness-policy surface is distributed to template consumers, the PR would ship references to a config file that is not present in a fresh checkout, causing `/track:init` / branch creation to fail as soon as they try to resolve the base branch. Add the config file to the tracked change or avoid making tracked workflow docs depend on it.

The changed `/track:done` adapter still contains the workflow steps inline and does not name an Operational SSoT workflow file at the top. The harness-policy adapter-SSoT check requires `.claude/commands/track/*.md` adapters to point to an existing `.harness/workflows/...` file and avoid duplicating step/gate/state logic in the adapter, so this command can now drift independently from the workflow layer.

The changed `/track:merge` adapter also encodes the PR resolution, wait/merge sequence, and post-merge behavior directly, without an Operational SSoT reference to an existing `.harness/workflows/...` file. That violates the harness-policy adapter-SSoT requirement and leaves the merge command's branch-strategy behavior outside the workflow SSoT where future policy changes are supposed to land.

`.harness/custom/review-prompts/types.md` has inconsistent hexagonal-architecture citations: line 36 uses `§Port Placement` instead of the project's `§Port Placement Rules`, and line 69 cites `§Dependency Direction`, but `knowledge/conventions/hexagonal-architecture.md` only has `## Layer Dependencies`. That makes the reviewer guidance point at the wrong convention sections.

The convention still claims ADR changes are protected by `adr` semantic review for `contradiction / factual error / infeasibility`, but the new `adr` briefing no longer reports general factual-error or infeasibility categories. Since `plan-artifacts` was removed, this line now overstates the actual review mechanism and can let convention-violating ADRs pass despite the convention promising reviewer coverage. Update the convention to match `.harness/custom/review-prompts/adr.md` or add the missing categories there.

This reads `metadata.json` through `infrastructure::track::codec::decode`, which hard-rejects every `schema_version != 6`. The promised fallback to `main` therefore never runs for existing v5 tracks/fixtures (for example `track/items/cli-composition-split-presentation-layer-2026-06-21/metadata.json`), so the new branch-strategy lookup will fail with `ConfigLoad` on older tracks instead of being backward-compatible. The same bug is duplicated in [review_v2/shared.rs](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/shared.rs#L208) and [track/fixpoint_resolve.rs](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs#L318).

The change keeps `FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter` as a `DiffBaseResolverPort` implementation inside `cli_composition` and now extends it with `base_branch` state. Per ADR D7 / the cli_composition scope policy, secondary adapter implementations belong in `libs/infrastructure`; this layer should only construct and inject them. Move this port implementation to infrastructure and wire that adapter here.

`FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter` is a secondary adapter implemented inside `cli_composition`: the struct stores the configured base branch here and its impl of `DiffBaseResolverPort` performs the `.commit_hash`/git fallback behavior. Under the cli_composition scope policy and ADR D7, port implementations belong in `libs/infrastructure`; this layer should only construct and inject that adapter. Move this resolver implementation, including the new `base_branch` state, into infrastructure and wire it from here.

`FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter` is still defined in `cli_composition` and implements the usecase `DryApprovalFactoryPort`. Per the cli_composition scope policy and ADR D7, port adapter implementations belong in `libs/infrastructure`; this layer should only construct and inject the infrastructure adapter.

`fixpoint_resolve` constructs `FixpointResolveInteractor` and immediately invokes `.resolve(&cmd)`. That is a use-case invocation from the composition root; ADR D2 requires this layer to wire drivers/adapters only, with command execution handled by the driving adapter layer.

`dispatch_review_cycle` directly invokes the review use case via `cycle.review(&scope)` and `cycle.fast_review(&scope)`. This keeps command execution in `cli_composition` instead of wiring a driver and handing it to the CLI, which violates the scope's invoke-leak rule.

`dry_check_approved_outcome` and `dry_write_outcome` assemble user-facing command output strings in `cli_composition`. Rendering belongs in `cli_driver` per ADR D2/D3; this layer should return wired behavior/data, not format CLI text.

`run_codex_review_str` is a public crate wiring entry point returning `Result<CodexReviewOutcome, String>`; `run_claude_review_str` mirrors the same shape. The scope policy forbids `Result<_, String>` in public `cli_composition` APIs; use a typed error such as `ReviewSharedError` or `CompositionError`.

`TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` still invokes the use-case dry gate while composing the driver. The call to `dry_gate.resolve_dry_gate(...)` performs the dry-gate interactor work before `FixpointResolveDriver` is returned, so this public composition method is still doing use-case execution at wiring time rather than only constructing an object graph. Per the cli_composition severity policy, this remains an invoke leak; move the dry-gate command/interactor into the returned driver or another driver-layer boundary so invocation happens only when the driver is run.

`FixpointResolveDriver::run` renders the user-facing fixpoint command output inside `cli_composition` by converting the `FixpointStep` into strings and returning a `CommandOutcome`. Rendering/output templating is owned by `cli_driver` under ADR D2/D3 and the scope policy; this driver should return or hand off raw result data and let `cli_driver` format `run-dfp`, `run-rfp scopes=...`, etc.

`ReviewCycleDriver::run` is still defined and executed inside `cli_composition`, and it directly invokes the review use case via `cycle.review(&scope)` / `cycle.fast_review(&scope)`. Wrapping the call in a private driver inside the composition crate does not satisfy the D2 composition-root boundary: `cli_composition` should wire a `cli_driver` driving adapter and hand it off, not invoke the use case itself. See ADR D2 and `hexagonal-architecture.md` §CLI as Composition Root.

`FixpointResolveDriver::run` is a public driver implemented in `cli_composition` and directly invokes both `resolve_dry_gate` and `FixpointResolveInteractor::resolve`. Per ADR D2 and `hexagonal-architecture.md` §CLI as Composition Root, the composition crate should only construct the object graph and inject it into a driving adapter from `cli_driver`; use-case execution should not live in `cli_composition`.

`render_verdict_payload` still assembles and serializes the command output payload in `cli_composition` before it is returned as stdout. Rendering/output templating belongs in `cli_driver` per ADR D2/D3, so the review-v2 dispatch path still has a render leak.

`TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` still performs use-case work: it calls `dry_gate.resolve_dry_gate(...)` and later `interactor.resolve(&cmd)` instead of only assembling the `FixpointDryGateCommand` / `FixpointResolveCommand` data and returning a wired driver. That keeps invocation inside `cli_composition` and violates the composition-root boundary the scope brief calls for.

P1 invoke leak: `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` still invokes the wired use cases inside `cli_composition`: it calls `dry_gate.resolve_dry_gate(...)` here and then `interactor.resolve(&cmd)` below. Per the cli_composition policy, this layer should only construct and inject the graph into a driver; the driver/usecase boundary should perform the invocation.

P1 invoke leak: `run_review_cycle` directly dispatches the usecase `ReviewCycle` with `cycle.review(&scope)` (and the fast branch calls `cycle.fast_review(&scope)`). Moving the call into a private helper still leaves usecase invocation in `cli_composition`; this should be injected into a driver/service boundary rather than invoked from the composition root module.

This struct still implements `DryApprovalFactoryPort` inside `cli_composition`. That is a secondary adapter implementation in the composition root; per D7 it belongs in `libs/infrastructure`, not here.

`dry_check_approved_outcome` and `dry_write_outcome` still assemble user-facing stdout/stderr strings in the composition root, and `dry.rs` returns them directly from production paths. Rendering belongs in `cli_driver`, not `cli_composition`.

`format_fixpoint_step` is still producing the CLI command strings in the composition root, and `fixpoint_resolve` returns the rendered string directly via `CommandOutcome::success(...)`. That is presentation logic and should live in `cli_driver`.

P1 render leak: `track_branch_create` still assembles the user-facing success text inside `cli_composition` via `CommandOutcome::success(Some(format!(...)))`. Under the cli_composition policy and ADR D2/D3, this layer should wire the operation and return a typed outcome for `cli_driver` to render, not template CLI output itself.

This fallback uses `repo_root` directly, but `JsonConfigBranchStrategyAdapter::new` rejects a symlinked trusted root. `dry_write` and `dry_check_approved` already compute `canonical_root`; passing the non-canonical path here will make metadata-missing tracks fail on symlinked checkouts instead of resolving the branch strategy config.
